title: hippocrepis scabra subsp. rupestris
intro: Hippocrepis scabra subsp. rupestris, also known as the rock hairy-leafed broom or rock hairy broom, is a plant species belonging to the legume family. Traditionally, Hippocrepis scabra subsp. rupestris has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which have been reported to aid in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and relieving spasms in the respiratory tract, as well as acting as an expectorant to help clear mucus and phlegm from the lungs. Hippocrepis scabra subsp. rupestris is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and interactions with certain medications.
